# Found Room

> A private community for experienced founders — not a course, coaching program, or mastermind, but an honest room where founders can set down what they're carrying and find peers who understand what that actually means.

Found Room was created by Daniel Fox, who scaled a company to 180 people, exited, and spent years navigating a disorientation that most founder communities don't name. The community draws on The Identity Cost — a body of work examining how founders route personal needs through business systems — as one available lens, not a curriculum.

The structure includes retreats, peer cohorts, and an ongoing private community. What happens inside them is determined by who shows up.

## Pages

- [Home](https://foundroom.co/): Overview of Found Room — what it is, what founders find here, and how to express interest in founding membership.
- [About](https://foundroom.co/about.html): Daniel Fox's story — how Found Room came to exist and the personal and intellectual foundation behind it.
- [The Room](https://foundroom.co/the-room.html): What membership looks like — the ongoing community, peer cohorts, and the structure founders enter.
- [Retreats](https://foundroom.co/retreats.html): Found Room's in-person retreat experiences for founders.
- [Journal](https://foundroom.co/journal.html): Writing from Daniel Fox on founders, identity, and the questions Found Room was built to hold.

## Writing

- [The Identity Cost](https://foundroom.co/identity-cost.html): The central body of work — examining how founders route personal needs through business systems, and what it costs them in clarity, relationships, and the quality of their lives.
- [What the Business Was Doing](https://foundroom.co/essay-what-the-business-was-doing.html): An essay on the unconscious functions founders assign to their companies, and what becomes visible when they stop.

## Contact

- Book a call: https://calendly.com/danielchristopherfox/30-minute
- Email: daniel@foundroom.co
